mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and fixed investment, which boosts corporate profit growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes above 330, further increases in the federal funds rate force the central bank to prioritize price stability over growth, causing market participants to price in a soft landing and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises during CPIAUCSL > 330, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and higher capacity utilization, which improves near-term corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following INDPRO increases, or the signal autocorrelation exceeds 0.8, indicating pure noise.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150 thousand confirm robust labor demand and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 150 thousand, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, stagflationary pressures erode corporate profit margins and force monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when GDPC1 growth falls below 2% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 320, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market regime below 4.0% unemployment, rising industrial production signals supply-side resilience that offsets wage inflation concerns, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize in >65% of periods when INDPRO rises during UNRATE < 4.0%, or the regime filter adds no incremental predictive power over a baseline model.
